steps.title: Identify the volume formula for a cylinder
steps.content: The volume of a right circular cylinder equals the base area times the height. We are given that the volume is $391$ cubic centimeters and the base area is $17$ square centimeters.

steps.title: Substitute and solve for height
steps.content: Substitute the known values into the formula: $$391 = 17h$$. Divide both sides by 17: $$h = \frac{391}{17} = 23$$. The height of the cylinder is 23 centimeters.
